The Help (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $169,705,587Details
International Box Office $43,414,417
Worldwide Box Office $213,120,004
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $92,540,642
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $30,158,224
Total $122,698,866
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Eugenia “Skeeter” Phelan just graduated from Ole Miss and is intent on getting a job as a writer. Unlike the girls she grew up with in Jackson, Mississippi, Skeeter wants a career and is quite content to put marriage and children on hold, much to her married friends’—and her mother’s—constant consternation. When she lands a job writing the Miss Myrna cleaning-hints column for the local newspaper, she seeks help from Aibileen, her best friend’s maid, and finds herself embarking on a clandestine project, spurred on by a book editor in New York and inspired by the moving stories she uncovers.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$26,044,590 (15.3% of total gross)
Legs:6.52 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:79.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 8.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,534 opening theaters/3,014 max. theaters, 9.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $255,948,246

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 10th, 2011 (Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: September 1st, 2011 (Wide) (Australia)
September 15th, 2011 (Wide) (New Zealand)
October 28th, 2011 (Wide) (Spain)
January 6th, 2012 (Wide) (Uruguay)
February 3rd, 2012 (Wide) (Brazil)
Video Release: December 6th, 2011 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic material.
(Rating bulletin 2161, 3/2/2011)
Running Time: 137 minutes
Award nomination: 2012 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Writers Guild of America Best Original Screenplay Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: African Americans, Bigotry, Boss From Hell, Non-Chronological, Writing and Writers, You Can't Go Home Again
Social setting: Upstairs / Downstairs
Subgenre: Historical Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: DreamWorks Pictures, Participant Media, Reliance BIG Entertainment, Image Nation Abu Dhabi, 1492 Pictures, Harbinger Pictures
Production Countries: United States, United Arab Emirates
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Eugenia “Skeeter” Phelan just graduated from Ole Miss and is intent on getting a job as a writer. Unlike the girls she grew up with in Jackson, Mississippi, Skeeter wants a career and is quite content to put marriage and children on hold, much to her married friends’—and her mother’s—constant consternation. When she lands a job writing the Miss Myrna cleaning-hints column for the local newspaper, she seeks help from Aibileen, her best friend’s maid, and finds herself embarking on a clandestine project, spurred on by a book editor in New York and inspired by the moving stories she uncovers.

Opening Weekend:$26,044,590 (15.3% of total gross)
Legs:6.52 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:79.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 8.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,534 opening theaters/3,014 max. theaters, 9.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $255,948,246

Languages: English
